# Introduction to Responsible AI for Young Innovators

## Course Overview

This 3-week introductory course is designed for high school STEM club students with no prior coding experience. "Introduction to Responsible AI for Young Innovators" aims to demystify Artificial Intelligence (AI) by exploring its fundamental concepts, ethical considerations, and societal impact. Through interactive discussions and hands-on activities using free online tools, students will gain a high-level understanding of AI models, identify potential biases and privacy concerns, and develop a framework for discussing the responsible use of AI technologies. The course emphasizes active participation and critical thinking to foster a generation of young innovators who are mindful of the ethical dimensions of technology.

## Learning Objectives

Upon successful completion of this course, students will be able to:

*   **Define** what AI models are at a high level, understanding their basic function and purpose.
*   **Identify** at least two potential sources of bias and unfairness in AI systems.
*   **Explain** at least two common privacy concerns associated with the development and deployment of AI.
*   **Articulate** key considerations for the acceptable and ethical use of AI.
*   **Participate constructively** in discussions about the societal implications of AI.
